steps.title: Construct the confidence interval
steps.content: Subtract and add the margin of error: $0.62-0.05=0.57$ and $0.62+0.05=0.67$. So the plausible range is 0.57 to 0.67.

steps.title: Interpret what the interval means
steps.content: Any proportion from 0.57 to 0.67 is plausible. Proportions below 0.57 or above 0.67 are not supported by the sample data.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It is plausible that the proportion is between $0.57$ and $0.67$.: This exactly describes the interval. ✓
  • Choice B — It is plausible that the proportion is less than $0.57$.: Values below 0.57 are outside the interval. ✗
  • Choice C — The proportion is exactly $0.62$.: 0.62 is the point estimate, not guaranteed to be the true population proportion. ✗
  • Choice D — It is plausible that the proportion is greater than $0.67$.: Values above 0.67 are outside the interval. ✗
That matches Choice A — It is plausible that the proportion is between $0.57$ and $0.67$..
